Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Rejection sampling can be inefficient if the proposal distribution has a much wider range than the target distribution.
S2 : Gibbs sampling involves iterating over all variables in a multivariate distribution in a fixed sequence.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Rejection sampling can be inefficient if the proposal distribution is too broad compared to the target distribution, leading to many rejections. So, S1 is correct.
• Gibbs sampling involves iterating over variables in a fixed sequence, sampling from their conditional distributions to approximate the joint distribution. So, S2 is correct.

S1 : Primary index must be sparse.
S2 : Secondary index over key must be dense.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Primary index on key with ordering may be dense or sparse.
• Secondary index over key build based on unordered field so that it is dense.

Q23 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Star schema has denormalized dimensions, leading to redundancy.
S2 : Snowflake schema reduces redundancy by normalizing dimensions.
S3 : Fact constellation schema cannot share dimension tables.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Star schema typically has denormalized dimension tables, which can lead to redundancy. So, S1 is correct

• Snowflake schema normalizes dimension tables, reducing redundancy. So, S2 is correct.
• Fact constellation schema can share dimension tables. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about the optimization problem in SVMs is/are correct?
S1 : The objective of the SVM optimization problem is to maximize the margin between the support vectors.
S2 : The SVM optimization problem with a linear kernel involves minimizing a convex function subject to linear constraints
S3 : The dual problem of the SVM optimization involves maximizing the Lagrangian dual function.
S4 : The SVM optimization problem does not include a regularization term.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The goal of the SVM optimization problem is to maximize the margin between the support vectors. So, S1 is true.
• The SVM optimization problem with a linear kernel involves minimizing a convex function with linear constraints. So, S2 is true.
• The dual problem of SVM optimization involves maximizing the Lagrangian dual function.
So, S3 is true.
• The SVM optimization problem includes a regularization term, especially in the soft-margin case. So, S4 is false.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In systematic sampling, every element in the population has an equal probability of being selected.
S2 : Systematic sampling is often used when the population is arranged in some specific order.
Which of the above is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Systematic sampling does not give each element an equal chance of selection due to the fixed periodic interval. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Systematic sampling is often used when the population has a specific order or structure. So, S2 is correct.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

When using the Gini index for decision tree splitting, missing values are typically handled by:
S1 : Ignoring them completely during the split calculation.
S2 : Assigning them to the most common class in the dataset.
S3 : Imputing missing values based on the values from similar records.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Missing values in decision trees can be handled by assigning them to the most common class (S2) or by imputing them based on similar records (S3). Ignoring them completely is not a common approach as it might lead to biased results.

Q35 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about the Gini index used in decision tree algorithms:
S1 : The Gini index is prone to bias when there are missing values in the dataset.
S2 : Missing values have no effect on the Gini index calculation as it only considers the distribution of classes in the nodes.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(a)
The Gini index is indeed prone to bias in the presence of missing values. Missing values can affect the purity measure of the nodes, leading to biased decision boundaries if not handled properly. On the other hand, statement S2 is incorrect because missing values do affect the calculation of the Gini index; appropriate handling or imputation is needed to ensure accurate results.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Logistic regression is primarily used for binary classification problems and estimates the probability of a data point belonging to one of two classes. So, option (a) is correct.
• Multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs) are typically used for supervised learning tasks such as classification and regression, not clustering tasks in unsupervised learning. So, option (b) is Incorrect.
• MLPs use activation functions (like ReLU or sigmoid) to introduce non-linearity into the model, which helps the network learn complex patterns. So, option (c) is correct.
• Logistic regression is not used for dimensionality reduction. It is mainly a classification
algorithm used for predicting binary outcomes. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q43 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1: Hierarchical clustering merges or splits clusters based on the distance between points or clusters.
S2: Naive Bayes classifier can handle both continuous and categorical features effectively.
S3: K-means clustering is a supervised learning method used for classification tasks.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(a)
• Hierarchical clustering can either merge clusters (agglomerative) or split clusters (divisive) based on distance metrics between points or clusters. So, S1 is correct.
• Naive Bayes classifier can handle both continuous features (using distributions like Gaussian) and categorical features effectively. So, S2 is correct.
• K-means clustering is an unsupervised learning method used for clustering tasks, not a supervised method for classification. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q44 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

What is the main advantage of the A* search algorithm over Dijkstra’s algorithm?
S1 : A* search algorithm uses heuristics to guide the search, making it faster in practice for many problems.
S2 : A* search algorithm can find solutions for problems that Dijkstra’s algorithm cannot handle.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The use of heuristics in A* helps it focus on the most promising paths, potentially reducing the number of nodes explored compared to Dijkstra’s algorithm. So, S1 is correct.
• A* and Dijkstra’s algorithms can solve the same types of problems; the difference lies in the efficiency and approach. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q45 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Identify the functionality of the following code when function returns the value 1. [“head” reference point to the first node of non-empt]

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
P1 (slower reference) starts at head and moves to the next node in each iteration.
P2 (faster reference) starts at head and moves to node ahead in each iteration.
If exists a cycle in the linked list (non-empty) it returns 1 by checking (P1 == P2) otherwise it returns 0.

NAT

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following first order logic statements, where are predicates. Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
Since ∀ is fully distributive over ∧, (a) and (b) are true.
Similarly ∃ is fully distributive over ∨, which makes (c) and (d) true as well.
Therefore all are true.

Q54 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
I. A prime attribute of a relation schema R is an attribute that appears in all candidate key of R.
II. If every candidate key of a relation is simple, then it will always be in 3NF.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
I. A prime attribute of a relation schema R is an attribute that appears in some candidate key of R.
II. If every candidate key of a relation is simple, then it will always be in 2NF but not always be in 3NF because transitive dependency may be present.

Q55 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1 : K-means clustering performs clustering based on minimizing variance within clusters.
S2 : K-means clustering does not require specifying the number of clusters in advance.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(a)
• K-means clustering aims to minimize the variance within clusters, typically measured as the sum of squared distances between points and their respective cluster centroids. So, S1 is correct.
• K-means clustering does require specifying the number of clusters (k) in advance, which is one of its limitations. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q56 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• UCS generally requires more memory than BFS because UCS stores nodes with their path
costs in a priority queue, while BFS stores nodes level-by-level. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• UCS finds the shortest path when all edge costs are non-negative. So, option (b) is correct.
• BFS does not use heuristics; it expands nodes level-by-level. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• DFS is complete in a finite search space if the goal is reachable, but it may fail in infinite spaces or if the goal is not reachable. So, option (d) is correct.

Which of the following statements are false?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(a) False: Imaginary eigen values always comes in pair, since eigen values are root of characteristics equation which always comes in pair
(b) False: Determinant is always non-negative, which does include 0, and it is not same as positive
(c) False: 0 vector is always a trivial solution to, where A has linearly dependent columns
(d) True: These set of matrices satisfies all properties of sub-spaces

Q62 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• Ridge regression adds a regularization term proportional to the square of the coefficients (L2 norm), which helps prevent overfitting. So, option (a) is correct.
• Ridge regression adds a regularization term proportional to the square of the coefficients (L2 norm), which helps prevent overfitting. So, option (b) is correct.
• K-means clustering does not use hierarchical decomposition. It requires the number of clusters (k) to be specified beforehand, and it iteratively partitions the data. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Gradient Boosting builds a sequence of models where each model attempts to correct the errors made by the previous models, typically using gradient descent to minimize the loss.
So, option (d) is correct.
